message.content: Find the latest CVE security vulnerability for Vue.js framework core (vuejs/core, not Vue CLI) in 2025 or 2026. Give me: CVE ID, affected versions, description of the flaw, and fix/patch version. Be brief.

input.query: Vue.js core vuejs/core CVE security vulnerability 2025 2026

content.text:
Title: Security - Overview · vuejs/core - GitHub
URL: https://github.com/vuejs/core/security
Published: N/A
Author: vuejs
Highlights:
Overview · vuejs/core · GitHub

/ core Public

Star 53.4k

- Pricing
- Notifications
- Fork 9.1k

## Security

Report a vulnerability

### SECURITY.md

# Reporting a Vulnerability

To report a vulnerability, please email security@vuejs.org.

While the discovery of new vulnerabilities is rare, we also recommend always using the latest versions of Vue and its official companion libraries to ensure your application remains as secure as possible.

Please note that we do not consider XSS via template expressions a valid attack vector, because it can only happen if the user intentionally uses untrusted content as template compilation source. This is similar to knowingly pasting untrusted scripts into a browser console. We explicitly warn users against using untrusted content as template compilation source in our documentation.

## Security Hall of Fame

We would like to thank the following security researchers for responsibly disclosing security issues to us.

- Jeet Pal - @jeetpal2007| Email| LinkedIn
- Mix - @mnixry
- Aviv Keller - @RedYetiDev| LinkedIn redyetidev@gmail.com
- Ben Zimmermann - @bzzimmy| Email

## There aren’t any published security advisories

---

| CVE | Vendors | Products | Updated | CVSS v3.1 | CVE-2025-5897 | 1 Vuejs | 1 Vue Cli | 2025-07-10 | 4.3 Medium |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A vulnerability was found in vuejs vue-cli up to 5.0.8. It has been rated as problematic. This issue affects the function HtmlPwaPlugin of the file packages/@vue/cli-plugin-pwa/lib/HtmlPwaPlugin.js of the component Markdown Code Handler. The manipulation leads to inefficient regular expression complexity. The attack may be initiated remotely. |
| CVE-2023-5718 | 1 Vuejs | 1 Devtools | 2024-11-21 | 4.3 Medium |
| The Vue.js Devtools extension was found to leak screenshot data back to a malicious web page via the standard `postMessage()` API. By creating a malicious web page with an iFrame targeting a sensitive resource (i.e. a locally accessible file or sensitive website), and registering a listener on the web page, the extension sent messages back to the listener, containing the base64 encoded screenshot data of the sensitive resource. |

Title: chore(deps): update dependency lodash to v4.18.1 [security] · Pull Request #14675 · vuejs/core
URL: https://github.com/vuejs/core/pull/14675
Published: 2026-04-03T05:40:27.000Z
Author: renovate[bot]
Highlights:
## chore(deps): update dependency lodash to v4.18.1 [security]
[...]
- Author: @renovate[bot]
- Association: CONTRIBUTOR
- State: open
- Labels: dependencies
- Source branch: renovate/npm-lodash-vulnerability
- Target branch: main
- Mergeable: unstable
- Commits: 1
- Additions: 4
- Deletions: 4
- Changed files: 1
- Created: 2026-04-03T05:40:27Z
- Updated: 2026-04-08T03:14:50Z
[...]
| Package | Change | Age | Confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| lodash (source) | `4.17.23` → `4.18.1` | [Image: age | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/lodash/4.18.1?slim=true] | [Image: confidence | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/lodash/4.17.23/4.18.1?slim=true] |
[...]
### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts
[...]
#### CVE-2026-2950
[...]
Lodash versions 4.17.23 and earlier are vulnerable to prototype pollution in the `_.unset` and `_.omit` functions. The fix for CVE-2025-13465 only guards against string key members, so an attacker can bypass the check by passing array-wrapped path segments. This allows deletion of properties from built-in prototypes such as `Object.prototype`, `Number.prototype`, and `String.prototype`.
[...]
This issue is patched in 4.18.0.
[...]
#### CVE-2026-4800
[...]
The fix for CVE-2021-23337 added validation for the `variable` option in `_.template` but did not apply the same validation to `options.imports` key names. Both paths flow into the same `Function()` constructor sink.
[...]
When an application passes untrusted input as `options.imports` key names, an attacker can inject default-parameter expressions that execute arbitrary code at template compilation time.
[...]
Additionally, `_.template` uses `assignInWith` to merge imports, which enumerates inherited properties via `for..in`. If `Object.prototype` has been polluted by any other vector, the polluted keys are copied into the imports object and passed to `Function()`.
[...]
### Patches
[...]
Users should upgrade to version 4.18.0.
[...]
The fix applies two changes:
[...]
1. Validate `importsKeys` against the existing `reForbiddenIdentifierChars` regex (same check already used for the `variable` option)
2. Replace `assignInWith` with `assignWith` when merging imports, so only own properties are enumerated
[...]
Do not pass untrusted input as key names in `options.imports`. Only use developer-controlled, static key names.
[...]
**Full Changelog**: 

##### Security
[...]
**`_.unset` / `_.omit`**: Fixed prototype pollution via `constructor`/`prototype` path traversal (GHSA-f23m-r3pf-42rh, fe8d32e). Previously, array-wrapped path segments and primitive roots could bypass the existing guards, allowing deletion of properties from built-in prototypes. Now `constructor` and `prototype` are blocked unconditionally as non-terminal path keys, matching `baseSet`. Calls that previously returned `true` and deleted the property now return `false` and leave the target untouched.
[...]
**`_.template`**: Fixed code injection via `imports` keys (GHSA-r5fr-rjxr-66jc, CVE-2026-4800, 879aaa9). Fixes an incomplete patch for CVE-2021-23337. The `variable` option was validated against `reForbiddenIdentifierChars` but `importsKeys` was left unguarded, allowing code injection via the same `Function()` constructor sink. `imports` keys containing forbidden identifier characters now throw `"Invalid imports option passed into _.template"`.
[...]
fallen out of
[...]
> hi, we've been researching application and repo health across the open source ecosystem, building a tool called repOptics and hoping to understand common patterns across healthy and functional repos/projects. ran a scan on vuejs/core and noticed that security signals here come primarily through Renovate dep bumps rather than proactive source scanning (no CodeQL or similar SAST in the CI pipeline).

---

Title: CVE Request: Remote Code Execution in Vue.js 3.x SSR Runtime Compiler (GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f) · Issue #7299 · github/advisory-database
URL: https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7299
Published: 2026-04-04T06:30:17.000Z
Author: sgInnora
Highlights:
## CVE Request: Remote Code Execution in Vue.js 3.x SSR Runtime Compiler (GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f)
[...]
- Author: @sgInnora
- State: open
- Created: 2026-04-04T06:30:17Z
- Updated: 2026-04-10T02:57:44Z
[...]
## CVE Request — Vendor Unresponsive
[...]
I am requesting GitHub CNA to assign a CVE ID for a critical vulnerability in Vue.js that the vendor has not responded to after 10 days.
[...]
- **GHSA**: GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f (submitted 2026-03-29, still in triage)
- **MITRE**: Ticket #2014701 (submitted 2026-03-25) + CVE Form Ticket #2019581 (submitted 2026-04-03)
- **Vendor**: security@vuejs.org — notified 2026-03-25, final notice 2026-03-31, **zero response**
[...]
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Product** | Vue.js (vuejs/core) — @vue/server-renderer, @vue/compiler-ssr, @vue/compiler-core |
| **Versions** | All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30 (latest) |
| **CWE** | CWE-94 (Improper Control of Generation of Code) |
| **CVSS** | 10.0 Critical (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H) |
| **Type** | Server-Side Template Injection → Remote Code Execution |
[...]
Vue 3's SSR runtime template compiler (`ssrCompile.ts`) executes compiled templates via `Function('require', code)(require)` without sandboxing. The `prefixIdentifiers` transform rewrites `Identifier` AST nodes but **not** `ThisExpression` nodes, allowing `this.__proto__.constructor.constructor(...)()` to achieve arbitrary code execution in Node.js.
[...]
- **CVE-2019-19919** (Handlebars.js): Identical exploit vector — prototype chain traversal to Function constructor in a template engine. Was assigned CVE.
- **CVE-2018-6341** (Vue 2 SSR): Related Vue 2 XSS vulnerability in SSR, was assigned CVE.
[...]
Any Vue 3 SSR application that compiles templates at runtime with user-controlled content is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution. This includes CMS platforms, low-code builders, and multi-tenant SaaS applications.
[...]
| Date | Action |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-03-25 | Reported to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-03-25 | MITRE ticket #2014701 submitted |
| 2026-03-29 | GitHub PVR GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f submitted |
| 2026-03-31 | Final notice sent to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-04-03 | MITRE CVE Form submitted (Ticket #2019581) |
| 2026-04-04 | **This GitHub CNA request** |
| 2026-06-23 | 90-day public disclosure deadline |
[...]
Please assign a CVE ID for this vulnerability. The vendor (Vue.js / Evan You) has been completely unresponsive for 10 days across multiple channels (direct email, GitHub PVR, MITRE).
[...]
> Hi GitHub Security team,
> 
> This CVE request was submitted on April 4, 2026 (6 days ago). The underlying vulnerability — a critical RCE (CVSS 10.0) in Vue.js 3.x SSR runtime compiler — was originally reported to the Vue.js security team (security@vuejs.org) on March 25, 2026, with zero response after 16 days.
> 
> Could you kindly provide a status update on this CVE assignment request? The 90-day coordinated disclosure deadline is June 23, 2026.
> 
> The GHSA ID is GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f. All technical details, PoC, and root cause analysis were included in the original submission.
> 
> Thank you,
> Feng Ning — Innora (innora.ai)

Title: chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security] · Pull Request #14460 · vuejs/core
URL: https://github.com/vuejs/core/pull/14460
Published: 2026-02-19T22:11:31.000Z
Author: renovate[bot]
Highlights:
# Pull Request: vuejs/core #14460
[...]
## chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security]
[...]
- Author: @renovate[bot]
- Association: CONTRIBUTOR
- State: merged
- Labels: dependencies
- Source branch: renovate/npm-minimatch-vulnerability
- Target branch: main
- Mergeable: unknown
- Commits: 1
- Additions: 3
- Deletions: 19
- Changed files: 2
- Created: 2026-02-19T22:11:31Z
- Updated: 2026-02-24T03:49:31Z
- Closed: 2026-02-24T03:49:30Z
- Merged: 2026-02-24T03:49:30Z
- Merged by: @edison1105
[...]
This PR contains the following updates:
[...]
| Package | Change | Age | Confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| minimatch | `~10.1.2` → `~10.2.0` | [Image: age | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/minimatch/10.2.1?slim=true] | [Image: confidence | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/minimatch/10.1.2/10.2.1?slim=true] |
[...]
### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts
[...]
#### CVE-2026-26996
[...]
`minimatch` is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) when a glob pattern contains many consecutive `*` wildcards followed by a literal character that doesn't appear in the test string. Each `*` compiles to a separate `[^/]*?` regex group, and when the match fails, V8's regex engine backtracks exponentially across all possible splits.
[...]
The time complexity is O(4^N) where N is the number of `*` characters. With N=15, a single `minimatch()` call takes ~2 seconds. With N=34, it hangs effectively forever.
[...]
When minimatch compiles a glob pattern, each `*` becomes `[^/]*?` in the generated regex. For a pattern like `***************X***`:
[...]
When the test string doesn't contain `X`, the regex engine must try every possible way to distribute the characters across all the `[^/]*?` groups before concluding no match exists. With N groups and M characters, this is O(C(N+M, N)) — exponential.
[...]
Any application that passes user-controlled strings to `minimatch()` as the pattern argument is vulnerable to DoS. This includes:
[...]
- File search/filter UIs that accept glob patterns
- `.gitignore`-style filtering with user-defined rules
- Build tools that accept glob configuration
- Any API that exposes glob matching to untrusted input
[...]
### Release Notes
[...]
**isaacs/minimatch (minimatch)**
[...]
### `v10.2.1`
[...]
### `v10.2.0`
[...]
### `v10.1.3`
[...]
**renovate[bot]** pushed commit `bfb92e0`: chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security]; force-pushed the branch · Feb 24, 2026 at 3:47am

Title: CVE Request: Cross-Site Scripting via isOn() bypass in Vue.js 3.x SSR (GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq) · Issue #7300 · github/advisory-database
URL: https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7300
Published: 2026-04-04T06:30:41.000Z
Author: sgInnora
Highlights:
## CVE Request: Cross-Site Scripting via isOn() bypass in Vue.js 3.x SSR (GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq)
[...]
- Author: @sgInnora
- State: open
- Created: 2026-04-04T06:30:41Z
- Updated: 2026-04-10T02:57:53Z
[...]
I am requesting GitHub CNA to assign a CVE ID for a high-severity vulnerability in Vue.js. The vendor has not responded after 10 days. This is a separate vulnerability from the RCE reported in github/advisory-database#7299.
[...]
- **GHSA**: GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq (submitted 2026-03-31, still in triage)
- **MITRE**: Ticket #2013988 (submitted 2026-03-25) + CVE Form (submitted 2026-04-03)
- **Vendor**: security@vuejs.org — notified 2026-03-25, **zero response**
[...]
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Product** | Vue.js (vuejs/core) — @vue/shared, @vue/server-renderer |
| **Versions** | All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30 (latest) |
| **CWE** | CWE-79 (Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation) |
| **CVSS** | 7.2 High (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N) |
| **Type** | SSR Cross-Site Scripting — Regression of CVE-2018-6341 |
[...]
The `isOn()` function in `packages/shared/src/general.ts:15` uses a case-sensitive check that only filters event attributes starting with `on` followed by an uppercase letter (e.g., `onClick`). However, HTML event attributes are case-insensitive, so `onclick`, `ONCLICK`, `oNcLiCk` all bypass the filter and render in SSR HTML output.
[...]
- `onClick` → filtered ✓
- `onclick` → **bypasses filter** ✗ (browser still executes)
- `ONCLICK` → **bypasses filter** ✗
[...]
21 out
[...]
1 DOM event
[...]
- **CVE-2018-6341** (Vue 2.x SSR v-bind XSS): The same class of vulnerability in Vue 2, fixed in v2.5.17. Vue 3's fix is incomplete — it only blocks camelCase event attributes, not lowercase/uppercase variants.
[...]
| Date | Action |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-03-25 | Reported to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-03-25 | MITRE ticket #2013988 submitted |
| 2026-03-31 | GitHub PVR GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq submitted |
| 2026-04-03 | MITRE CVE Form submitted |
| 2026-04-04 | **This GitHub CNA request** |
| 2026-06-23 | 90-day public disclosure deadline |
[...]
Please assign a CVE ID for this vulnerability. This is a distinct vulnerability (XSS, different root cause and CWE) from the RCE reported in #7299. The vendor has been completely unresponsive across all channels.
[...]
> Hi GitHub Security team,
> 
> Follow-up on this CVE request submitted April 4, 2026. This is a companion vulnerability to GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f (#7299) — an SSR XSS via isOn() regex bypass in Vue.js 3.x (CVSS 7.2).
> 
> The Vue.js security team has not responded to either vulnerability report after 16 days. Requesting CVE assignment to proceed with coordinated disclosure.
> 
> GHSA ID: GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq
> 
> Thank you,
> Feng Ning — Innora (innora.ai)

---

This PR contains the following updates:
[...]
| Package | Change | Age | Adoption | Passing | Confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vitest (source) | `3.0.2` -> `3.0.5` | [Image: age | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/vitest/3.0.5?slim=true] | [Image: adoption | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/adoption/npm/vitest/3.0.5?slim=true] | [Image: passing | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/compatibility/npm/vitest/3.0.2/3.0.5?slim=true] | [Image: confidence | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/vitest/3.0.2/3.0.5?slim=true] |
[...]
### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts
[...]
#### CVE-2025-24964
[...]
Arbitrary remote Code Execution when accessing a malicious website while Vitest API server is listening by Cross-site WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH) attacks.
[...]
When `api` option is enabled (Vitest UI enables it), Vitest starts a WebSocket server. This WebSocket server did not check Origin header and did not have any authorization mechanism and was vulnerable to CSWSH attacks.
https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/blob/9a581e1c43e5c02b11e2a8026a55ce6a8cb35114/packages/vitest/src/api/setup.ts#L32-L46
[...]
This WebSocket server has `saveTestFile` API that can edit a test file and `rerun` API that can rerun the tests. An attacker can execute arbitrary code by injecting a code in a test file by the `saveTestFile` API and then running that file by calling the `rerun` API.
[...]
1. Open Vitest UI.
2. Access a malicious web site with the script below.
3. If you have `calc` executable in `PATH` env var (you'll likely have it if you are running on Windows), that application will be executed.
[...]
This vulnerability can result in remote code execution for users that are using Vitest serve API.
[...]
### Release Notes

 
 vitest-dev/vitest (vitest) 

### `v3.0.5`
[...]
- Validate websocket request - by @​hi-ogawa and @​AriPerkkio in https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/issues/7317  (191ef)
- Don't toggle cli cursor on non-TTY - by @​AriPerkkio in https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/issues/7336  (3c805)
- **vite-node**: Differentiate file url with hash and query - by @​hi-ogawa in https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/issues/7365  (926ca)
[...]
### `v3.0.4`
[...]
### `v3.0.3`
[...]

### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts
[...]
#### CVE-2025-31125
[...]
### Summary

The contents of arbitrary files can be returned to the browser.
[...]
Only apps explicitly exposing the Vite dev server to the network (using `--host` or `server.host` config option) are affected.
[...]
- base64 encoded content of non-allowed files is exposed using `?inline&import` (originally reported as `?import&?inline=1.wasm?init`)
- content of non-allowed files is exposed using `?raw?import`
[...]
't needed to reproduce the issue for files inside the project root.
[...]
#### CVE-2025-31486
[...]
The contents of arbitrary files can be returned to the browser.
[...]
Only apps explicitly exposing the Vite dev server to the network (using --host or server.host config option) are affected..
[...]
#### `.svg`

Requests ending with `.svg` are loaded at this line.
[...]
/037f801075ec35bb6e52145d659f71a23813c48f/packages/vite/src/node/plugins/asset.ts#L285-L290
[...]
By adding `?.svg` with `?.wasm?init` or with `sec-fetch-dest: script` header, the restriction was able to bypass.
[...]
This bypass is only possible if the file is smaller than `build.assetsInlineLimit` (default: 4kB) and when using Vite 6.0+.
[...]
#### relative paths
[...]
The check was applied before the id normalization. This allowed requests to bypass with relative paths (e.g. `../../`).
[...]
### PoC
[...]
```bash
npm create vite@latest
[...]
cd vite-project/
[...]
send request to read `etc/passwd`
[...]
```bash
curl 'http://127.0.0.1:5173/etc/passwd?.svg?.wasm?init'
```
[...]
```bash
curl 'http://127.0.0.1:5173/@​fs/x/x/x/vite-project/?/../../../../../etc/passwd?import&?raw'
```

Based on the search results, there are two critical unpatched CVE requests for Vue.js core (vuejs/core) in 2026, but **no officially assigned CVE IDs yet** since the vendor hasn't responded. Here are the details:

## GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f (RCE in SSR Runtime Compiler)
- **Status:** CVE request pending (no CVE ID assigned)
- **Affected versions:** All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30
- **CVSS:** 10.0 Critical
- **Flaw:** Server-Side Template Injection → RCE. SSR runtime compiler executes templates via `Function('require', code)(require)` without sandboxing. The `prefixIdentifiers` transform doesn't rewrite `ThisExpression` nodes, allowing `this.__proto__.constructor.constructor(...)()` for arbitrary code execution in Node.js.
- **Fix/Patch:** None available yet (vendor unresponsive since March 25, 2026)

## GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq (SSR XSS via isOn() bypass)
- **Status:** CVE request pending (no CVE ID assigned)
- **Affected versions:** All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30
- **CVSS:** 7.2 High
- **Flaw:** The `isOn()` function uses case-sensitive checks filtering only `on[A-Z]...` pattern, but HTML event attributes are case-insensitive. `onclick`, `ONCLICK`, `oNcLiCk` bypass the filter and render in SSR HTML.
- **Fix/Patch:** None available yet (vendor unresponsive)

Both were reported to security@vuejs.org on March 25, 2026 with zero response. GitHub CNA CVE requests submitted April 4, 2026. No official CVE IDs have been assigned.

https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7299
https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7300
